tensions.text: The node's crossCheck cites IndexBox as the source for a 10–12% intelligent-PDU CAGR used to justify lowering the band top to €360M, but the only IndexBox source in this pull giving that 10–12% figure is the EU 'Power and Cable Management' report — a €8.5–9.5B market far broader than rack PDUs, not a rack-PDU-specific figure. The cross-check growth rate may not transfer cleanly to the €300M rack-PDU base.

Growth-rate estimates for ostensibly overlapping 'PDU market' scopes vary wildly by source: Grand View global rack PDU 9.7% CAGR (2026-2033) vs. BIS/R&M Europe PDUs+PSUs 21.23% CAGR vs. MarketsAndMarkets global Data Center PDU 19.5% CAGR vs. R&M Europe PDU (volume-based) 8.9% CAGR vs. IndexBox EU rack power distribution panels 5–8% CAGR vs. Eastern Europe panels 9–12% CAGR. This 3–4x spread in growth rate directly undermines confidence in extrapolating the €300M anchor forward.

Germany alone is forecast to reach $509.6M in a broader 'PDU' category by 2031 (Research and Markets), which already exceeds the entire curated CE-7 rack-PDU base of €300M (2025) even before accounting for the other six CE-7 countries — suggesting the €300M/€360M ceiling may be too conservative if category boundaries are loosened even slightly from 'rack PDU' to 'PDU'.

The node explicitly discounts the BIS/R&M €2.45B (Europe PDUs+PSUs, 2024) figure as a contaminated 'PDUs+PSUs combined' ceiling, yet the same broader-category conflation problem recurs across nearly every other Europe-labeled source found (MarketsAndMarkets includes floor PDUs; IndexBox 'Power and Cable Management' includes non-PDU categories; IndexBox 'Rack Power Distribution Panels' may not equal 'Rack PDUs') — the definitional contamination the node tried to remove is pervasive across the entire evidence base, not isolated to the one source it flagged.

absences: No search result surfaced a directly-stated Europe-scoped (or CE-scoped) rack-PDU-only headline €/$ figure — the one report with the exact right scope (IndexBox EU Rack PDUs) has its headline number paywalled, exactly as the node's provenance notes.

No explicit 'Europe % of global rack-PDU market' figure was found in any source — only North America's 38% share is stated; the node's ~30% Europe estimate remains unconfirmed by any retrieved source.

No CE-7 country-level breakdown (Poland, Czech Republic, Hungary, Slovakia, Austria, Romania, Bulgaria or similar) for rack PDUs specifically was found; only Germany/UK/France figures for a broader 'PDU' category, and an Eastern-Europe-wide (not CE-7-scoped) panels figure.

No source reconciled the unit-volume-based Europe PDU sizing (2,662 thousand units in 2021, R&M) with any euro/dollar figure, leaving no way to cross-check the €300M value anchor against a volume-based benchmark.

No buyer-segment split (colocation vs. hyperscale vs. enterprise) was found for the rack-PDU-specific market at Europe or CE level — only a broader 'data center applications = 55–65% of demand' figure for the adjacent 'rack power distribution panels' category.
